quote:
We're renaming trainlint → hansard so the name matches its actual job as Second Foundation Labs' public-record harness for agent work, instead of mis-selling it as an ML-training linter.DID / BUILT (newest first):Caught and fixed a `getSessCookie` infinite recursion in the local worker deployment (`report-server/local.sh`, `0887fe1`) before it reached Cloudflare; bisect table shows `3b64ed4` clean and `263149d` 500s, fixed in `b66946e`.Verified the old `trainlint_token` cookie still authenticates `200` against the renamed worker on local deployment, and widened `anon-suffix` `endsWith` checks to accept both eras (`b66946e`).Rebranded the portal live on `secondfoundationlabs.com` (`3b64ed4`, worker `572f6ae1@100%`): login title, Hansard card, and "The work on the record" tagline — no path or cookie changes, probes confirm.Set the rename boundary: only human-visible surfaces change (`/hansard:*` commands, repo, brand, `HANSARD_*` env vars with `TRAINLINT_*` fallback); the five state-carrying IDs (R2 bucket, cookie, `localStorage` key, data dirname, worker name) stay grandfathered permanently.NEXT:Settle the breakage-table boundary with the operator, then cut the remaining surface renames starting with brand strings since those carry zero risk.
DONEDONE = /hansard:plan works after reinstall; old TRAINLINT_ env and existing browser sessions still work; zero substrate/report/annotation loss; the other session lands its work without conflict.
●●●●●●●
